text.title: headless-cli-four-guards Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes, each one mapping to a missing guard.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.reason: CLI tries to start the TUI; emits terminal control sequences; the job hangs until job-level timeout.

section.p: Symptom: cron job hangs the full timeout window. Root cause: missing headless flag. Fix: add the CLI's headless flag; rely on lint to catch regressions.

antipattern.reason: Without allowlist, the agent stalls on the first permission prompt. With `--dangerously-skip-permissions` the agent runs arbitrary destructive tools.

section.p: Symptom: cron stalls OR cron pushes random commits. Root cause: guard #2 broken. Fix: narrow allowlist; never use `--dangerously-skip-permissions` in production.

antipattern.reason: Loop bugs drain budget within minutes. The cap exists to bound cost and to act as a tripwire when the loop logic regresses.

section.p: Symptom: daily budget spent in a single cron run. Root cause: missing or absurd cap. Fix: cap at the realistic workflow length (15-30); fail loud if hit.

antipattern.description: No `< /dev/null` on stdin (or worse, piped JSON in).
antipattern.reason: Agent's stdin inherits whatever the parent shell forwards (closed pipe, garbage, or interactive TTY); behaviour is non-deterministic. Piping JSON "just in case" violates the supported contract.

section.p: Symptom: non-deterministic behaviour across reruns. Root cause: open stdin. Fix: always `< /dev/null`; never pipe content into a `-p` invocation.
